question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription to a fee-schedule topic with startTime set to a future timestamp (exploiting a clock skew between client and server), causing the isValidStartTime() check to pass while the service waits for messages that include fee updates not yet finalized?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.rate()] Can an unprivileged external user flood a fee-schedule topic subscription to cause the rate() calculation (1000.0 * count.get() / elapsed) to overflow an int, producing a negative or zero rate value that corrupts the finished() log entry and obscures evidence of fee-message manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnSubscribe/doFinally hooks to cause subscriberCount to become negative by triggering an exception between incrementAndGet() and decrementAndGet(), causing the Gauge metric to report incorrect subscriber counts and masking a fee-message replay attack?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where numMissingMessages equals Long.MAX_VALUE by controlling the sequence numbers of current and last messages, causing the newFilter.limit to be set to Long.MAX_VALUE and triggering an unbounded database query for fee-schedule messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(() -> incomingMessages(topicContext)) lazy evaluation to subscribe multiple times to the same TopicContext, causing the count and last state to be shared across multiple live streams and corrupting the sequence-number tracking for fee-schedule messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the grpcProperties.isCheckTopicExists() == false configuration by supplying a topicId that does not exist in the entity repository, causing the service to create a synthetic Entity with type TOPIC and stream fee-schedule messages for a non-existent topic, bypassing entity validation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the takeWhile(t -> t.getConsensusTimestamp() < filter.getEndTime()) boundary condition (strict less-than) to cause the service to exclude a fee-schedule message whose consensusTimestamp equals endTime, resulting in an incomplete fee update being applied?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the newFilter construction in incomingMessages() (filter.toBuilder().limit(limit).startTime(startTime).build()) to bypass the isValidStartTime() validation on the rebuilt filter, since the toBuilder() path may not re-run @AssertTrue validators, allowing a future startTime for fee-message retrieval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the newFilter built in missingMessages() uses toBuilder() without re-validating @AssertTrue constraints, allowing endTime to be set to current.getConsensusTimestamp() which could be less than startTime, causing an invalid gap-fill query that retrieves fee messages from an unintended time range?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AtomicReference<TopicMessage> last field in TopicContext by causing concurrent onNext() calls (via Flux.merge) to race on last.set(topicMessage), resulting in a lost update where a fee-schedule message's sequence number is not properly recorded, breaking the deduplication invariant?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a fee-schedule topic with a very small endTime window (e.g., 1 nanosecond range) to cause the safetyCheck Mono.delay(Duration.ofSeconds(1L)) to fire after the subscription has already completed, triggering a spurious gap-recovery query that re-delivers fee messages to a stale context?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endTimeInterval addition in isComplete() (filter.getEndTime() plus grpcProperties.getEndTimeInterval()) to keep a subscription alive beyond the intended endTime, receiving fee-schedule messages that were published after the design-parameter cutoff?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter(t -> last == null || t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) deduplication to suppress a fee-schedule message with a lower sequence number that was legitimately re-issued after a network fork, causing the fee update to be permanently skipped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the concatWith ordering (historical.concatWith(live)) to receive a fee-schedule message from the historical retriever and then receive a conflicting version of the same message from the live listener, causing the downstream processor to apply two different versions of the same fee update?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set topicId to a fee-schedule topic's EntityId with a crafted shard/realm/num combination that maps to a different entity in the repository, causing the service to stream fee messages from the wrong topic and apply incorrect fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Schedulers.boundedElastic() thread pool used by the safetyCheck subscription to cause thread starvation, delaying the delivery of fee-schedule messages to legitimate subscribers and creating a window where stale fee parameters remain in effect?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the duplicate-message suppression path (numMissingMessages <= -1) in missingMessages() by crafting a scenario where a fee-schedule message appears to have a lower sequence number than last, causing the service to silently drop a legitimate fee update?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the log.info() call in subscribeTopic() that logs the full filter object to inject log-forging characters into the subscriberId field, obscuring audit evidence of a fee-schedule message manipulation attack?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicMessageFilter.subscriberId is generated client-side (RandomStringUtils.random) and passed in the filter to inject a crafted subscriberId that matches a pattern used by monitoring systems, causing fee-message delivery events to be misattributed in metrics?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the takeUntilOther(pastEndTime(topicContext)) operator by crafting an endTime that causes pastEndTime() to emit immediately, terminating the live stream before a fee-schedule message is delivered, resulting in an incomplete fee update?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TopicContext.finished()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the finished() callback with SignalType.CANCEL by abruptly cancelling a fee-schedule topic subscription at the exact moment a fee update is being processed, leaving the fee state partially updated outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Validated annotation on TopicMessageServiceImpl to cause a ConstraintViolationException during filter validation that is not properly handled, resulting in a partial subscription state where the subscriberCount is incremented but never decremented, exhausting resources for fee-message delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RepeatSpec.create(r -> !topicContext.isComplete(), Long.MAX_VALUE) in pastEndTime() to cause the repeat loop to run Long.MAX_VALUE times when isComplete() never returns true, consuming all available CPU and preventing fee-schedule message processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the limit recalculation (filter.getLimit() - topicContext.getCount().get()) in incomingMessages() when count has been incremented by the historical retriever, causing the live listener to receive fewer fee-schedule messages than intended and missing a fee update?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.justOrEmpty(entityRepository.findById(topicId.getId())) call in topicExists() to cause a database connection exhaustion by opening many concurrent subscriptions, preventing the entity lookup for fee-schedule topics and causing EntityNotFoundException to suppress fee updates?
